name: Flickering Flame
effect:
You create a small flame in your palm. It provides light at a close range. You can throw the flame as a projectile.
Weak. Deals +2 fire damage.
Strong. Deals +4 fire damage and the target suffers burning (2) for a short duration.
Critical. Deals +6 fire damage and the target suffers burning (4) for a short duration.
You can also use the flame to ignite flammable materials in range, such as tinder or straw.

name: Frost Snap
effect:
By snapping your fingers, you explosively chill the air around the target.
Weak. You deal +2 frost damage.
Strong. You deal +4 frost damage and the target is briefly slowed.
Critical. You deal +6 frost damage and the target is briefly dazed and slowed.
You can also cast this spell to cool down an object, such as extinguishing the last embers of a campfire.

name: Static Spark
effect:
You generate a small electric discharge from your fingertip against a target.
Weak. Deal +2 lightning damage.
Strong. Deal +4 lightning damage to the target and you can deal +2 lightning damage to one other creature in melee range of the target.
Critical. Deal +6 lightning damage to the target and you can deal +4 lightning damage to one other creature in melee range of the target.
You can also use the flame to ignite flammable materials in range, such as tinder or straw.

name: Chromatic Orb
effect:
You focus elemental energy into a concentrated orb and hurl it against a target. When casting the spell, choose one damage type between acid (ignore 1/2 AV), fire (burning (2/4/6)), frost (briefly slowed), lightning (briefly staggered), or poison (briefly poisoned).
Weak. Deals +4 damage of the chosen damage type and apply the additional effect mentioned for that damage type.
Strong. Deals +8 damage of the chosen damage type and apply the additional effect mentioned for that damage type.
Critical. Deals +12 damage of the chosen damage type and apply the additional effect mentioned for that damage type.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +6/12/18 damage instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +8/16/24 damage instead.

name: Elemental Ward
effect:
You surround yourself in a ward that absorbs elemental energies affecting you. Cast this spell before taking damage or on your turn.
On a success, you briefly gain resistance against acid, fire, frost, lightning, and poison damage.
heightened:
(Rank 2) While the spell lasts, inflict half of the acid, fire, frost, lightning, or poison damage you take (after AV and resistances) from an attacker back to them (ignore AV).
(Rank 3) While the spell lasts, inflict the same amount of the acid, fire, frost, lightning, or poison damage you take (after AV and resistances) from an attacker back to them (ignore AV) instead.

name: Flame Burst
effect:
Holding your open hand out, a wide burst of fire erupts from it.
Weak. Deals +2 fire damage to all targets.
Strong. Deals +4 fire damage to all targets.
Critical. Deals +6 fire damage to all targets and each of them suffers burning (2) for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +4/8/12 damage against each target instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +6/12/18 damage against each target instead.

name: Flaming Weapon
effect:
Enchant your weapon with magical flames.
On a success, the weapon emits bright light in melee range and dim light in close range of it and deals +2 fire damage. You can make a weapon attack as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The weapon deals +4 fire damage instead.
(Rank 3) The weapon deals +6 fire damage instead.

name: Frozen Weapon
effect:
Enchant your weapon with magical frost.
On a success, the weapon deals +2 frost damage. On a strong or critical hit, the target is briefly slowed. You can make a weapon attack as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The weapon deals +4 frost damage instead.
(Rank 3) The weapon deals +6 frost damage instead.

name: Ice Shards
effect:
You form an icicle in your grasp. The icicle explodes on impact, sending out razor sharp shards around it.
Weak. Deal +4 frost damage to the target.
Strong. Deal +8 frost damage to the target.
Critical. Deal +12 frost damage to the target.
Also on a success, the icicle explodes upon impact, dealing +2 frost damage to all other creatures in melee range of the target.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +6/12/18 damage to the target and +4 damage with the explosion instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +8/16/24 damage to the target and +6 damage with the explosion instead.

name: Lightning Arc
effect:
Arcs of lightning spring forth from your fingertips, jumping from creature to creature.
Weak. Deal +2 lightning damage up to three creatures close to each other.
Strong. Deal +4 lightning damage up to three creatures close to each other.
Critical. Deal +6 lightning damage up to three creatures close to each other.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +4/8/12 damage to each target instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +6/12/18 damage to each target instead.

name: Lightning Weapon
effect:
Enchant your weapon with magical frost.
On a success, the weapon deals +2 lightning damage. On a strong or critical hit, the target is briefly staggered. You can make a weapon attack as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The weapon deals +4 lightning damage instead.
(Rank 3) The weapon deals +6 lightning damage instead.

name: Scorching Ray
effect:
You form three flaming orbs hovering before you. From each orb, you can shoot a beam of scorching flame. You can choose the same or different targets and roll one attack for each beam.
Weak. The beam deals +2 fire damage.
Strong. The beam deals +4 fire damage.
Critical. The beam deals +6 fire damage.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Each beam deals +4/8/12 damage instead.
(Rank 3) Each beam deals +6/12/18 damage instead.

name: Fireball
effect:
You channel a violent sphere of flame, pulsing with raw elemental energy. On a success, you throw it towards the target location where it explodes upon impact.
This spell targets every creature in close range of the target location. Use your roll’s result vs. Dodge.
Weak. Deals +4 fire damage to all targets and each of them suffers burning (2) for a short duration.
Strong. Deals +8 fire damage to all targets and each of them suffers burning (4) for a short duration.
Critical. Deals +12 fire damage to all targets and each of them suffers burning (6) for a short duration.
heightened: (Rank 3) Deal +6/12/18 damage to all targets instead and increase the burning to (4/6/8).

name: Frost Wave
effect:
You conjure a chilling wave of frost that emanates an icy blast in front of you.
Weak. Deal +4 frost damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +8 frost damage to each target and they are briefly slowed.
Critical. Deal +12 frost damage to each target and they are slowed for a short duration. They can roll Strength + Fortitude at the end of their turns to end the effect early.
heightened: (Rank 3) Deal +6/12/18 damage to each target instead. On a critical success, the slowed condition lasts for a medium duration instead.

name: Ice Lance
effect:
You form a long spear-like ice shard, hovering above your hand. On a success, you can choose to use the ice lance as a melee weapon or throw it against a target within range. The ice lance lasts for a short duration or until destroyed.
Weapon. Use your roll’s result as a melee attack against a target in reach using the ice lance. The ice lance deals +5 frost weapon damage, has the fragile, pierce, reach, and versatile (+1) properties, and d6 Durability. You can use your spell power instead of your melee power for the base damage with this weapon. On your following turns, you can attack with it again by rolling Mind + Arcana.
Throw. Use your roll’s result vs. Dodge against a single target in range.
Weak. Deal +6 frost damage.
Strong. Deal +12 frost damage.
Critical. Deal +18 frost damage.
heightened: (Rank 3) The ice lance deals +7 frost weapon damage instead. When thrown, deal +8/16/24 damage instead.

name: Lightning Strike
effect:
You summon a powerful strike of lightning that streaks forth in a straight line from you to a target point, electrifying every creature it passes.
Weak. Deal +4 lightning damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +8 lightning damage to each target and they are briefly staggered.
Critical. Deal +12 lightning damage to each target and they are briefly staggered.
heightened: (Rank 3) Deal +6/12/18 damage to each target instead. On a strong or critical success, targets are briefly stunned instead of staggered.

name: Prismatic Missile
effect:
You split raw elemental energy through a prism of will and loose a beam that shimmers with two colors at once. When casting the spell, choose two damage types among acid, fire, frost, lightning, and poison. The beam's damage counts as both chosen types. The target only applies resistance against this damage if they are resistant to both chosen types, and applies weakness if they are weak to either.
Weak. Deal +6 damage of the chosen types.
Strong. Deal +12 damage of the chosen types.
Critical. Deal +18 damage of the chosen types. The target also suffers the condition associated with one of the chosen types (acid: ignore 1/2 AV on this damage, fire: burning (2), frost: briefly slowed, lightning: briefly staggered, poison: briefly poisoned).
heightened:
(Rank 3) Deal +8/16/24 damage instead.
(Rank 4) Deal +10/20/30 damage instead.
(Rank 5) Deal +12/24/36 damage instead.

name: Black Flame Bolt
effect:
You conjure an unnatural black flame which relentlessly consumes everything it touches.
Weak. Deal +6 fire damage and the target suffers burning (2).
Strong. Deal +12 fire damage and the target suffers burning (4).
Critical. Deal +18 fire damage and the target suffers burning (6).
The black fire ignores fire resistance and reduces immunity against fire damage to a resistance against it. Burning from black fire can‘t be put out by mundane means and only does, once the caster of it is unconscious or dead. Alternately it can be put out by magic that removes a magical effect to be stopped.

name: Horrific Vision
effect:
You conjure false visions into the target‘s mind, making them project horrific events of them or their loved ones.
Weak. Deal +0 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Strong. Deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is briefly frightened.
Critical. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is frightened for a short time. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Maddening Whispers
effect:
You produce unintelligible voices of madness into the target’s mind, distracting them and breaking their mental fortitude.
Weak. Deal +0 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Strong. Deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is briefly distracted by the whispers.
Critical. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is distracted by the whispers for a short time. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Minor Illusion
effect:
You can create a stationary image of a small or medium sized creature or object. On a success, your chosen illusion appears at the target location in range. Attempts to physically interact with the illusion automatically reveal it to be an illusion.
The illusion lasts for a short duration or until you move out of range of it. You can immediately recast this spell as an Action at the end of the duration to maintain the illusion for a longer time.

name: Color Spray
effect:
You conjure a cloud of flashing multi-colored lights from your palm. Target all creatures in the spell’s area.
Weak. Each target is briefly blinded.
Strong. Each target is briefly blinded and dazed.
Critical. Each target is briefly blinded and stunned.

name: Disguise Form
effect:
You transform your image through illusions, appearing as another similar sized creature.
On a success, choose any humanoid form with the same size as yourself. You can also choose any style of clothing or other worn items as part of this spell.
You assume your chosen form through illusion. Any parts that appear larger or smaller than your original form aren‘t solid and will be translucent when in contact with other solid matter.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.

name: False Enemy
effect:
Using an illusionary creature, you fool your target into believing they are attacked by the illusion. Choose a small or medium sized form for your false enemy. On a success, the target imagines the false enemy to be attacking them in melee.
Weak. Deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Strong. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is briefly distracted by the false enemy.
Critical. Deal +6 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is briefly distracted by the false enemy. The target’s roll to see through the false enemy’s illusion is hard.
You can use your Quick Action on your next turn to repeat the roll for this spell without spending additional Focus. On a success, apply the spell’s effects again. On a failure or if you choose not to use your Action to continue the spell, the spell ends and you can’t continue to cast it without spending additional Focus.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +4/+8/+12 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +6/+12/+18 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Illusory Trap
effect:
You make a creature believe that they triggered a trap.
On a success, they believe they set off the trap and are getting pinned by it. Choose any plausible form for the illusory trap to take (e.g. hunting trap or trap hole).
Weak. Deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Strong. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is grappled by the illusory trap.
Critical. Deal +6 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is grappled and restrained by the illusory trap.
After the target believes it was pinned by the trap, the illusion lasts for a short duration or until they see through it.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +4/+8/+12 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +6/+12/+18 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Mirror Image
effect:
You create illusionary duplicates of yourself, mimicking your actions to confuse enemies about your true position. Cast this spell only during your turn.
On a success, you create three duplicates of yourself that stay at your side and mimic your movements.
When a creature attempts to target you with an attack, spell, or other effect, roll 1d12. While you have three duplicates, a result of 4 or higher redirect’s the effect to one of your duplicates. With two duplicates, you need a 5 or higher and with only one duplicate you need a 7 or higher.
Each duplicate has a general Defense of 7 + your Arcana. If they hit a duplicate, it disappears.
Otherwise the duplicates last for up to a short duration.

name: Hallucinated Swarm
effect:
Create an illusory swarm of creatures or projectiles that a group of creatures believe to be attacked by. Target all creatures in the spell’s area.
Weak. Deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) to each target.
Strong. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) to each target and they are briefly confused.
Critical. Deal +6 psychic damage (ignore AV) to each target and they are confused for a short time. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.
heightened: (Rank 3) Deal +4/+8/+12 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Illusionary Terrain
effect:
Create a large scale illusion, blending into the environment by mimicking the surrounding scenery.
Choose any inanimate object, structure, or environmental feature that encompasses up to a close three dimensional area.
On a success, the illusion appears and lasts for a medium duration, even if you move out of range of it.
heightened: (Rank 3) The illusion can cover up to a short distance in all three dimensions instead.

name: Invisibility
effect:
You shroud yourself in illusions of your surroundings, hiding you from the sights of others. Cast this spell on your turn.
On a success, you turn invisible. While invisible, you are hidden from any creature which hasn’t successfully rolled against this spells illusion or has an innate ability to see through invisibility.
This spell lasts for a short duration or until you attack or cast another spell. You can immediately recast this spell as a Quick Action at the end of the duration to remain invisible for a longer time.
heightened: (Rank 3) You can remain invisible up to a medium duration instead.

name: Major Illusion
effect:
You summon the image of an object, creature, or other visible phenomenon, which can be large or smaller. I appears absolutely real, mimicking sounds, smells, movements, and even temperature (tough it won’t deal actual damage).
On a success, your chosen illusion appears at the target location in range.
While you are in range of the illusion, you can use your Quick Action to let the illusion change in any way or move within range. It will follow your instructions as long as it takes, so it can mimic walking to another location or having a conversation with another creature.
Attempts to physically interact with the illusion automatically reveal it to be an illusion. The illusion lasts for up to a short duration, even if you move out of range. You can immediately recast this spell as an Action at the end of the duration to maintain the illusion for a longer time.
heightened:
(Rank 3) The illusion can be of huge or smaller size and lasts up to a medium duration instead.
(Rank 4) The illusion can fill a close area with several moving, interacting elements, such as a market stall with a vendor or a campfire ringed by sleeping soldiers, and lasts up to a medium duration instead.

name: Misdirection
effect: You alter a creature’s perceived positioning and stance for just a moment to confuse an attacker. Choose any creature in range that is currently being attacked vs. their Parry or Dodge (after the attacker has rolled). On a success, reduce the SL of the attacker by one step (min. blunder).

name: Trap Room
effect:
You create a spacial illusion where a deadly trap or hazard springs into being. Target all creatures in close range of the target location.
On a success, each target believes they set off the trap and are getting pinned by it. Choose any plausible form for the illusory trap to take (e.g. crumbling ceiling or spikes from the ground).
Weak. Deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) against each target.
Strong. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) against each target and they are grappled by the illusory trap.
Critical. Deal +6 psychic damage (ignore AV) against each target and they are grappled and restrained by the illusory trap.
After a target believes they were pinned by the trap, the illusion lasts for a short duration or until they see through it.
heightened: (Rank 3) Deal +6/+12/+18 psychic damage (ignore AV) against each target instead.

name: Waking Dream
effect:
You trap a creature in their dreams. The target has to be of a tier equal to or lower than your Arcana. If the creature is at full HP, you suffer +1 bane on the roll.
On a success, the creature falls unconscious for a short duration. If any creature uses an Action to shake them, they take any damage, or are otherwise disturbed, they can roll Spirit + Perception vs. your Resist to break free of the illusion.
heightened: (Rank 3) The target remains unconscious for up to a medium duration instead.

name: Mislead
effect:
You turn invisible at the very same time as you create a mirror image of yourself, leading creatures to believe you still remain where you were.
On a success, you turn invisible and can immediately move a close distance (no Quick Actions can be used in response to this Movement). While invisible, you are hidden from any creature which hasn’t successfully rolled against this spells illusion or has an innate ability to see through invisibility.
You also create a duplicate at your previous location which remains for the spell’s duration. On your following turns, you can use your Quick Action to make the duplicate move a short distance, gesture, speak, or act in any other way you choose.
This spell lasts for a short duration or until you attack or cast another spell. You can immediately recast this spell as a Quick Action at the end of the duration to remain invisible for a longer time.

name: Phantasmal Killer
effect:
You force a creature to be surrounded by their nightmares and deepest fears, only perceivable by them.
Weak. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Strong. Deal +8 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Critical. Deal +12 psychic damage (ignore AV).
On a success, the target is also frightened for a short duration. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.
While frightened by this spell, you can use your Quick Action on your following turns to repeat the roll vs. Resist. On a success, deal another +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) to them.

name: Seeming
effect: You draw a shimmer of borrowed faces over your companions, and strangers look back where friends once stood. You cloak up to six creatures of your choice within range (including yourself) in an illusory disguise for a medium duration. You choose each creature's new appearance, altering face, build, clothing, and voice, and you may make a creature seem any height within their own Size category (a Medium creature can appear as any Medium build from short to tall, but cannot seem Small or Large). The illusion holds to the body and moves with it, but cannot mimic a specific real individual well enough to fool those who know them. A creature that has reason to doubt what they see may roll Insight vs. your casting result to see through one disguise.

name: Arcane Bolt
effect:
Produce a small arcane projectile and hurl it against a target.
Weak. Deals +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Strong. Deals +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and the target is briefly dazed.
Critical. Deals +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and the target is briefly dazed.

name: Arcane Glyph
effect:
Conjure a small arcane glyph that is almost invisible on a stationary object or surface you touch. On a success, choose one of the following effects:
Explosion. Roll vs. Dodge targeting each creature close to the glyph. On a hit, each target takes +2/4/6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Alarm. A loud continuous sound blasts from the glyph, hearable anywhere within a very long range of it.
Spell Effect. Choose one of your other non-ritual spells targeting one or multiple creatures in no more than short range. Immediately spend the Focus required to cast the spell. Once the glyph activates, cast the chosen spell with random targets within range.
Also determine a condition for when the glyph is supposed to activate, such as somebody touching it, stepping on it, or moving directly past it. The condition has to be met in close range of the glyph.
Once the condition is met, the glyph erupts with the chosen effect.

name: Extraplanar Pocket
effect:
Choose one item taking up 1 load or less and hold it out before you. Cast this spell on your turn.
On a success, your hand reaches into an extraplanar pocket, either storing the item you are holding or withdrawing an item you stored beforehand from it during your turn.
You can only ever store items with a combined load of up to 2 + Arcana in the extraplanar pocket. When storing supply in it, you can‘t store more than a d12 (or 5 units) for any single type of supply in it.
Once you are unconcious, any stored item re-appears before you.

name: Alarm
effect:
You mark an area with an arcane sigil that functions as an alarm.
When beginning to cast the spell, choose an opening or passage way no larger than a close distance. You can also choose individual creatures which should be ignored by the alarm and if the alarm activates audibly or mentally.
On a success, you set an alarm that activates once a tiny or larger creature moves through or touches the enchanted area given your specified instructions.
A mental alarm is only perceptibly by you while you are no more than an hour’s walk away from the alarm and even wakes you up if you are sleeping.
An audible alarm briefly produces the sound of a bell which is hearable within of medium distance of the alarm.
The alarm lasts until triggered by a creature or for a long duration.

name: Arcane Barrier
effect:
You summon an arcane barrier of semi-translucent energy that provides cover and let’s nothing through. The barrier takes the dimensions of a rectangular door (2 meters x 1 meter) and can be oriented in any direction.
On a success, you create the barrier within range in the desired orientation, as long as it touches some solid surface or object. Standing behind the barrier provides cover.
The barrier is immovable and lasts for a short duration or until destroyed. The barrier can be destroyed like any other object. It has 6 Parry and Dodge, 4 AV, and 20 HP.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The barrier has 8 Parry and Dodge, 6 AV, and 30 HP instead.
(Rank 3) The barrier has 10 Parry and Dodge, 8 AV, and 40 HP instead.

name: Arcane Missiles
effect:
Conjure one or multiple small projectiles or pure arcane energy and lunge them against a number of creatures.
Weak. You fire one missile.
Strong. You fire two missiles.
Critical. You fire three missiles.
Choose any number of creatures in range you can see as targets to divide the missiles between. The missiles ignore cover and can take any clear path towards the targets. Each missile automatically hits, counting as a weak success, and deals +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
heightened:
(Rank 2) Each missile deals +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.
(Rank 3) Each missile deals +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.

name: Conjure Familiar
effect:
You set up a ritual to summon an arcane familiar from unknown places beyond this world. As part of casting this spell, you must also spend 100 coins in incense and other occult ingredients. Choose your familiars physical appearance from the list of animal traits in the Companion section.
On a success, you summon your arcane familiar in the chosen form (it has the statistics of a tier 0 companion).
You have a psychic connection to your familiar, as long as the two of you are on the same sphere of existence. This connection manifests itself as an intuitive sharing of emotions. You can use this connection to tell your familiar what to do and where to go nonverbally. You can also spend your turn meditating to fully experience all senses of your familiar. While meditating this way, you are considered unconscious and are unaware of your own body‘s surroundings.
Any creature capable of sensing magical auras will notice the extraplanar nature of your familiar. Most civilized folks will see this as unnatural and evil. The familiar remains until killed, but can be re-summoned with another ritual. You can choose a new form for your familiar each time you cast this spell.

name: Hale of Blades
effect:
You conjure a hail of energy-made blades hovering around you. You can send out the blades, whirling and striking at your targets.
Weak. Deal +2 damage up to three creatures close to each other.
Strong. Deal +4 damage up to three creatures close to each other.
Critical. Deal +6 damage up to three creatures close to each other.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +4/8/12 damage to each target instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +6/12/18 damage to each target instead.

name: Infuse Item
effect:
You touch any medium or smaller item, temporarily infusing it with arcane energy.
On a success, the item increases it’s Durability die by one step. Apply one of the following effects based on the type of item it is:
- Armor, Shield, or Helmet: It grants +1 AV (enhancement bonus).
- Ammo: It deals +2 damage.
- Weapon: Increase it’s weapon damage by 1 (enhancement bonus).
- Spell Catalyst: Increase your spell damage by +1/2/3 (enhancement bonus).
- Backpack: While using this to carry items, it adds +2 to your carry capacity limits.
These effects last for a long duration. This spell counts as an enchantment for the type of item which you infused (for example, it counts as an enchant (weapon) spell effect for an infused weapon).
heightened:
(Rank 2) Depending on the type of item it grants +2 AV (enhancement bonus), +4 damage, weapon damage increased by 2 (enhancement bonus), increased spell damage by +2/4/6 (enhancement bonus), or +4 to your carry capacity limits respectively instead.
(Rank 3) Depending on the type of item it grants +3 AV (enhancement bonus), +6 damage, weapon damage increased by 3 (enhancement bonus), increased spell damage by +3/6/9 (enhancement bonus), or +6 to your carry capacity limits respectively instead.

name: Mage Armor
effect:
You weave a lattice of arcane force around your body that hardens into unseen armor.
On a success, you gain +2 AV (armor bonus) for a long duration. This bonus does not stack with other armor bonuses such as worn armor, Bone Armor, or the Mana Shield talent. Use only the highest. The armor holds until the duration ends, even after you take damage.
heightened:
(Rank 2) You gain +3 AV (armor bonus) instead.
(Rank 3) You gain +4 AV (armor bonus) instead.
(Rank 4) You gain +5 AV (armor bonus) instead.
(Rank 5) You gain +6 AV (armor bonus) instead.

name: Arcane Barrage
effect:
Conjure a barrage of small projectiles or pure arcane energy and lunge them against a number of creatures.
Weak. You fire three missile.
Strong. You fire four missiles.
Critical. You fire five missiles.
Choose any number of creatures in range you can see as targets to divide the missiles between. The missiles ignore cover and can take any clear path towards the targets. Each missile automatically hits, counting as a weak success, and deals +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
heightened: (Rank 3) Each missile deals +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.

name: Web
effect:
You hurl a mote of conjured matter that erupts into a mass of thick, sticky strands, filling the area with layered webbing.
On a success, webs fill an area close to the target location. The webbed area is difficult terrain. When a creature tries to move through the webs for the first time on their turn, or is pushed or pulled into them, they must succeed on a Strength + Athletics roll vs. this spell's target Difficulty or become grappled and restrained by the strands. A grappled creature can repeat the roll as an Action to break free. The webs must be anchored between at least two solid surfaces, such as walls, floor and ceiling, or trees, or they collapse and the spell fails. The webs are flammable. Any fire damage in the area burns them away in an instant, and each creature caught in the webs when they burn takes 4 fire damage (ignore AV). The webs last for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 3) The webs fill an area within short range of the target location and last for a medium duration instead.
(Rank 4) The webs fill an area within medium range of the target location and last for a medium duration instead.
(Rank 5) The webs fill an area within medium range of the target location and last for a long duration instead.

name: Mass Hold
effect:
You clench your outstretched hand and a crushing weight of will falls upon every mind before you. Target each hostile creature in a close area around the target location whose tier is equal to or lower than your Arcana, using your roll's result vs. their Resist.
On a success, each target is paralyzed as their body refuses every command. At the end of each of their turns, an affected creature can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist to end the effect early. Whenever an affected creature takes damage, they immediately make this roll as well. Creatures without a mind, such as most constructs and oozes, are unaffected. This spell lasts for a short duration.

name: Part Stone
effect:
Over long minutes of murmured intent you lay your hands upon the barrier and bend your will into it, and the matter loosens and draws aside as though it had never been solid.
Choose a section of mundane earth, stone, clay, brick, or wood you are touching, up to a close distance in thickness. The material must not be metal, magically created, or warded by any magical effect. The section must also be plain and free of attached mechanisms, fixtures, or moving parts, such as levers, hinges, sconces, or pipes, and it must not be load-bearing to the point that removing it would collapse the structure. On a success, the chosen material crumbles away into a heap of loose rubble and sand at the base of the passage, leaving an opening wide and tall enough for a medium creature to pass through walking upright. The opening is permanent. It is an ordinary hole in an ordinary wall and can only be closed again by mundane means, such as masonry or filling it in.

name: Arcane Circle
effect:
You summon a magical circle under your feet, depicting arcane sigils.
On a success, you summon the arcane circle in your melee range. Any creature casting an arcane spell while standing on the circle gains +1 boon when casting or concentrating on an arcane spell and deals +2 damage per SL with any arcane spell.
The arcane circle remains stationary at the position you cast it at for a medium duration, or until you cast another one at a different position.
heightened: (Rank 3) While standing in the circle, a creature deals +4 damage per SL with any arcane spell instead.

name: Arcane Eye
effect:
You create an invisible, magical eye hovering in the air. On a success, the eye appears at the target location.
You telepathically receive visual information from the eye. The eye has darkvision (medium/long) and moves by hovering. As an Action, you can move the eye a short distance in any direction. The eye can fit through any gap as small as one centimetre.
Creatures with abilities to sense creatures non-visually can perceive the eye. The eye has 8 Defense and 1 HP.
The eye lasts for a medium duration or until moved away more than one kilometre from you.

name: Eldritch Tendrils
effect:
You create small fractures in reality at the target location, spewing dark, unnatural tendrils covered in acidic ichor, which furiously lash at creatures in their reach. Target all enemies in close range of the target location.
Weak. Deal +4 acid damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target.
Strong. Deal +8 acid damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target and they are grappled by the tendrils. While grappled by them, the target takes 2 lasting acid damage.
Critical. Deal +12 acid (ignore 1/2 AV) damage to each target and they are grappled and restrained by the tendrils. While grappled by them, the target takes 4 lasting acid damage.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Quick Action to let the tendrils lash at all enemies in their reach again, but they stop grappling or restraining their previous target. Roll your spell attack again when doing so.
Then tendril’s area is considered difficult terrain. The tendrils stay at the target location for a short duration.
heightened: (Rank 3) Deal +6/12/18 acid damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead. The target takes 4 or 6 lasting acid damage on a strong or critical hit respectively instead.

name: Phase Step
effect:
You step through the veil of the material sphere, quickly teleporting yourself to a viable location you can see in range.
On a success, you re-appear at the target location without spending Movement.
heightened: (Rank 3) Increase this spell’s range to long.

name: Summon Aberration
effect:
You conjure a strange being from an unknown realm beyond the stars. Choose any aberration trait from the Mounts & Companions section for the creature.
On a success, an aberrant creature of the chosen type appears at any point within range.
Treat the creature as your companion (see Mounts & Companions for more). It is friendly towards you and your allies while you maintain concentration for this spell.
The creature has the statistics of a tier 2 companion with your chosen trait.
You can dismiss the creature as a Quick Action during your turn. Otherwise it remains until reduced to 0 HP or for a short duration.
If you lose concentration on this spell, the creature becomes hostile to you and your allies. Roll initiative for it and treat it as an enemy. You can’t dismiss the creature anymore and it remains until reduced to 0 HP or after the spell’s duration.
heightened: (Rank 3) You conjure an aberrant creature with the statistics of a tier 3 companion instead.

name: Arcane Blast
effect:
You form a dense swirling sphere of arcane energy and shoot it against a creature at rapid speed.
Weak. Deal +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Strong. Deal +12 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and the target is pushed a short distance.
Critical. Deal +18 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and the target is pushed a short distance.

name: Astral Gate
effect:
You open up an unstable hole into the astral void, sucking in creatures and potentially trapping them in the unknown dark.
On a success, you summon the gate within short distance of the target area. The gate’s area is considered difficult terrain. Target each creature in the gate’s range and use your roll’s result vs. their Parry.
On a hit, each target takes +8 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) is pushed a short distance into the center of the gate. If they are already within the center area of the gate, they take +8 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and fall prone instead. If they were already prone within the center of the gate, they are sucked into the astral void instead. Loose light objects and dead or unconscious creatures within the center of gate are also sucked into the astral void. Creatures within the astral void can breath but are restrained for the duration.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Action to repeat the roll vs. Parry against all creatures within the gate. The gate lasts for a short duration.
When the gate closes, all objects and creatures sucked into it re-appear.

name: Burst of Tendrils
effect:
A cluster of fleshy, razor-tipped tendrils erupt from your body and lash out at creatures in front of you. Target all creatures in the spell’s area.
Weak. Deal +6 damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +12 damage to each target and they suffer bleeding (4) until healed.
Critical. Deal +18 damage to each target and they suffer bleeding (6) until healed.

name: Dimension Door
effect:
You mentally focus on any location within range you can see or visualize because you were there before.
On a success, you open a glimmering door of arcane energy before you and step through to teleport to your chosen location. You must be able to move to step through the door.
You can bring along objects you are able to carry, as well as one willing creature or one creature you are grappling that is of equal or smaller Size than you.
If the target location is already occupied by a creature or object, you and any creature that moves with you instead take 10 psychic damage (ignore AV) and fail to teleport.

name: Wall of Force
effect:
You form a translucent wall of pure arcane force. Choose any shape for the wall in a continuous path along the ground with up to a medium distance from start to end within range. The wall also extends a short distance into the air.
On a success, the wall appears. Any creature standing in the walls path is pushed a close distance away from it.
The barrier is immovable and lasts for a medium duration or until destroyed. The barrier can be destroyed like any other object. It has 10 Parry and Dodge, 8 AV, and 40 HP.

name: Arcane Empowerment
effect:
You bolster yourself with raw arcane energy. On a success, you gain the following effects:
- Whenever you deal damage with an arcane spell, add your Arcana to the damage.
- Arcane spells cost -2 Focus (min. 0).
- You gain resistance against acid, blast, fire, frost, lightning, necrotic, poison, and radiant damage.
This spell lasts for a short duration. You can immediately recast this spell as an Action at the end of the duration to remain in this form for a longer time.
When the spell ends, the target suffers one Fatigue and is briefly paralyzed.

name: Astral Body
effect:
You loosen your soul from your flesh and step out of yourself, joined to your body by a thin silver cord only you can see.
On a success, your body falls unconscious where it rests and your astral form rises from it. Your astral form can fly with +1 Movement per turn, passes through solid objects and walls freely, and perceives its surroundings normally. You cannot touch, carry, or affect anything physical, you cannot cast spells, and you cannot speak to creatures, though creatures able to perceive spirits can see you and speak with you. Your astral form can travel up to an hour's walk away from your body. If your astral form takes damage from any effect that can strike spirits, you immediately snap back into your body and suffer one Fatigue. You can end the spell at will, returning to your body instantly. If your body is moved, disturbed, or takes damage while you are away, you feel it and can return to your body instantly. This spell lasts for a medium duration.

name: Force Cage
effect: You conjure an immovable prison of latticed arcane force around your target. On a success, a barred cage of force encloses a melee-range area at the target location, trapping every creature of large Size or smaller inside it. A trapped creature cannot leave the cage by any nonmagical means and cannot be pushed or pulled out of it. They can still act, attack, and cast through the bars. A trapped creature that tries to teleport or otherwise travel out by magic must first succeed on a roll vs. your Resist, or the attempt fails and is wasted. The cage is immovable, but it can be attacked and destroyed like any other object from either side, with 12 Parry, 12 Dodge, 10 AV, and 60 HP. It also ends if an effect that ends spells removes it, or when its duration ends. The cage lasts for a medium duration.

name: Teleportation Circle
effect:
Over hours of exacting ritual you inscribe a circle of arcane sigils into a solid floor, attuned to its one distant twin. Each circle is inscribed separately with its own casting of this spell, and each casting consumes 1500 coins worth of rare inks and powdered silver.
On a success, the circle is inscribed and attuned to its twin. You can maintain at most one linked pair. Inscribing a new circle beyond the pair causes your oldest circle to fade. The two circles can lie at most a week's travel apart. While both circles remain intact, you can activate the link as an Action while standing in either circle. The link holds until the start of your next turn, and during that time any creature that steps into one circle instantly steps out of the other. A circle is a physical inscription. Scratching it open, breaking the floor, or a single casting that ends spells destroys it, and destroying either circle severs the link.
heightened: (Rank 5) You may inscribe your circles anywhere within the same sphere of existence with no limit on the distance between them, and you are no longer bound to a single linked pair. You can maintain any number of your own circles at once and a creature stepping into one may step out of any other you choose. Each casting consumes 5000 coins worth of materials instead.

name: Detect Magic
effect:
You attune your vision to the lingering auras of magical influence.
On a success, you detect any magical auras within range emanated by creatures, items, or other magical effects, such as illusions.
Weak. You briefly know the general direction of each aura during the moment when this spell is cast.
Strong. Same as a weak success, and you keep track of all detected auras within range for a short duration.
Critical. Same as a strong success, and you know magical nature, the discipline, or tradition of the detected auras.

name: Mental Link
effect:
You focus on the mind of another creature you can see within range. Cast this spell on your turn.
On a failure, the target is aware of your attempt to pry their mind and you can‘t target them again with this spell for the rest of the scene.
On a success, you can either send a brief message, thought, or mental image to the target. Alternatively you can get insight into their current surface thoughts and emotions.
You automatically succeed when targeting a willing creature and they can return a brief message, thought, or mental image to you as a response.

name: Mind Blast
effect:
You concentrate to overwhelm a creature‘s mind with mental energy.
Weak. Deal +0 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Strong. Deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is briefly dazed.
Critical. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is dazed for a short time. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Attack Thoughts
effect:
You blast the thoughts of a creature, making them unable to think clearly.
Weak. Deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Strong. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is briefly confused.
Critical. Deal +6 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is confused for a short time. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Control Beast
effect:
You dominate the mind of an animal with no more than a d4 Mind you can see within range. The target has to be of a tier equal to or lower than your Arcana.
Weak. The target is briefly stunned.
Strong. You briefly take control of the target, choosing what they do on their next turn.
Critical. You take control of the target for a short duration, choosing what they do on their turns.
After the spell ends, the target is aware you influenced their mind. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude at the end of their turns to end the effect early. Once they do, you can no longer cast this spell against them for the rest of the scene.

name: Foresight
effect:
You heighten your senses, granting you an instinctual glimpse into the immediate future. Cast this when being attacked or on your turn.
Weak. Attacks against you briefly suffer +1 bane.
Strong. Attacks against you briefly suffer +1 bane and you briefly gain +1 boon on your next attack roll.
Critical. Attacks against you briefly suffer +1 bane, you briefly gain +1 boon and add your Arcana as damage on your next attack roll.

name: Psychometry
effect:
Touching an inanimate object, you gain insights into it’s recent history and what events took place around it.
Weak. You gain one Discovery.
Strong. You gain two Discovery.
Critical. You gain three Discovery.
You can spend one Discovery, the same as when rolling for Discovery, to gain insights into the objects history or what events took place around them. Both have to have taken place during the last week.
You can only cast this spell once per object.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Your insights can reach back up to one year.
(Rank 4) Your insights can reach back through the object's entire history, including the circumstances of its making.

name: Subtle Suggestion
effect:
You subtly plant a suggested action in a creature‘s mind.
Choose any simple action that doesn‘t inflict harm on the creature or one of it‘s allies.
Weak. The target suffers +1 bane on any action on their next turn, unless it is following the suggested action.
Strong. The target suffers +2 banes on any action on their next turn, unless it is following the suggested action.
Critical. The target is forced to take the suggested action on their next turn.
After the spell ends, the target is aware you influenced their mind. Once you fail to cast this spell against any creature, it is immune against it for the rest of the scene.

name: True Strike
effect:
You focus on a creatures thoughts, gaining insight into their defenses. Cast this spell on your turn.
On a success, increase the SL of your next attack until the end of your next turn by one step (max. critical success).

name: Counterspell
effect:
You invade the mind of a creature, interrupting them in the middle of casting a spell.
Cast this spell when a creature in range casts a spell. Add their spell‘s rank to their Resist against this spell.
Weak. You nullify the spell they were about to cast.
Strong. Deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) and you nullify the spell they were about to cast.
Critical. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) and you nullify the spell they were about to cast.

name: Forced Suggestion
effect:
You speak an word of command, forcing your target to take a certain course of action.
Choose any reasonable sounding course of action that doesn‘t inflict harm on the creature itself. If the creature is in combat with you or otherwise actively aggressive towards you, you suffer +1 bane on the roll.
On a success, the target is forced to pursue your suggested course of action without distraction.
This spell lasts until the course of action is complete or for a short duration. The target can roll Spirit + Insight vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early. The effect also ends early if the target takes damage from any source.
After the spell ends, the target is aware you influenced their mind.

name: Psychic Wave
effect:
You send out a wave of destructive mental energy.
Weak. Deal +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) against each target.
Strong. Deal +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) against each target and they are briefly dazed.
Critical. Deal +6 psychic damage (ignore AV) against each target and they are dazed for a short time. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Wave of Madness
effect:
You fling a cone of raw, screaming thought, and the minds it washes over buckle under visions that were never theirs. Target all creatures in the cone.
Weak. Deal +2 psychic damage to each target and they are briefly confused.
Strong. Deal +4 psychic damage to each target and they are briefly confused.
Critical. Deal +6 psychic damage to each target and they are confused for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +3/+6/+9 psychic damage to each target instead.
- Increase the spell's range to medium.

name: Invade Dreams
effect:
You lay a hand on the sleeper's brow and slip beneath their eyelids, walking the halls of their dreams as an unseen architect. You enter the dreams of a touched sleeping, intelligent creature. If they fail to resist, choose one effect for the duration of the dream:
- Plant a vision. You author a dream the creature will remember as a true omen or prophecy. On waking they believe the vision was genuine until events plainly contradict it.
- Sift the sleeping mind. Ask their dreaming self up to three questions. The GM answers with what the creature knows, one of: a place, a person, a past event, a fear, or a held secret. If they know nothing relevant, the dream turns evasive and you learn nothing from that question.
- Sow unrest. The creature suffers restless nightmares, waking unrested (they gain no benefit from the sleep) and frightened of a subject you weave into the dream for a short duration after waking.
The ritual takes minutes and requires the target to remain asleep throughout. A creature can attempt this against the same target only once per night.

name: Astral Body
effect:
You loosen your soul from your flesh and step out of yourself, joined to your body by a thin silver cord only you can see.
On a success, your body falls unconscious where it rests and your astral form rises from it. Your astral form can fly with +1 Movement per turn, passes through solid objects and walls freely, and perceives its surroundings normally. You cannot touch, carry, or affect anything physical, you cannot cast spells, and you cannot speak to creatures, though creatures able to perceive spirits can see you and speak with you. Your astral form can travel up to an hour's walk away from your body. If your astral form takes damage from any effect that can strike spirits, you immediately snap back into your body and suffer one Fatigue. You can end the spell at will, returning to your body instantly. If your body is moved, disturbed, or takes damage while you are away, you feel it and can return to your body instantly. This spell lasts for a medium duration.

name: Kinetic Pull
effect:
You telekinetically hold onto a target object or creature of medium or smaller size within range and violently pull them towards you.
When targeting an object held by a creature, you suffer +1 bane on the roll.
Weak. Pull the target up to a close distance towards you.
Strong. Pull the target up to a close distance towards you and they fall prone.
Critical. Pull the target up to a short distance towards you and they fall prone.
This Movement is treated the same as pushing in regards to Opportunity Attacks, larger creatures, and other effects.

name: Kinetic Push
effect:
You rapidly extend your open hand forward, shooting a blast wave against the target from it.
Weak. Deal +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Strong. Deal +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and push the target close.
Critical. Deal +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and push the target a short distance.

name: Weak Telekinesis
effect:
You hold out your hand and grasp at an object in range, slowly levitating it it into the air. The object can be of small or tiny size and take up to your carrying capacity in load. The object can be any loose object lying around, or ammo, a weapon, or another item you carry.
You can choose to either move or throw the object towards a target location.
Move. Roll vs. moderate difficulty (8). On a success, you move the object up to a short distance within range. You can choose to concentrate on this spell and hold your grasp on the object until your next turn. During your next turn, you can cast this spell again to move the object further. On a failure, you drop the object at it’s current location.
Throw. Roll vs. Dodge against a single target in range. On a hit, the object deals 2 weapon damage in addition to your spell base damage. If you throw a weapon, apply it’s properties and effects in addition to the spell effect.

name: Gravity Orb
effect:
You create a tiny orb of pure force at the target location, pulling everything around it towards it. This spell targets every medium or smaller sized creature in close range of the orb.
Weak. Each target is briefly slowed.
Strong. Each target is briefly grappled by the orb.
Critical. Each target is briefly grappled and restrained by orb.
The close area around the orb is considered difficult terrain. When any medium or smaller sized creature first enters the orb‘s range or starts it‘s turn there, they have to roll Strength + Athletics. On a failure, they are briefly grappled and restrained by the orb.
The orb lasts for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The orb affects large or smaller creatures and lasts a medium duration instead.
(Rank 3) The orb affects large or smaller creatures and lasts a medium duration, and its pull extends in short range of it instead: the whole area is difficult terrain, and creatures entering it or starting their turn there roll the entry save.

name: Reflective Barrier
effect:
You create a nearly invisible energy barrier around you, held up by your mental power.
On a success, the barrier grants you +4 AV (armor bonus) and lasts for a short duration.
While the barrier is active, when you are attacked by a ranged attack and they miss you, you can use a Quick Action to reflect their attack back on them. Roll Mind + Arcana vs. their Dodge. On a hit, resolve the attack’s normal effects.

name: Shockwave
effect:
You spread your arms out from you, sending a shockwave of kinetic energy in all directions.
Weak. Deal +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against all creatures and objects in range.
Strong. Deal +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against all creatures and objects in range and push them close away from you.
Critical. Deal +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against all creatures and objects in range, push them close away from you, and they fall prone.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +4/+8/+12 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +6/+12/+18 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead, and on a strong or critical success targets are pushed a short distance away from you.

name: Telekinetic Crush
effect:
You telekinetically grasp at a creature, suffocating them inside a giant, invisible hand.
Weak. Deal +4 crush damage.
Strong. Deal +8 crush damage.
Critical. Deal +12 crush damage.

name: Telekinetic Volley
effect:
You telekinetically take hold of multiple small items. Choose up to three tiny objects, such as ammo, stones, or household objects taking up 2 or less load each. The objects can be any loose object lying around, or ammo, a weapon, or another item you carry.
You can choose to either move or throw the objects towards a target location.
Move. Roll vs. moderate difficulty (8). On a success, you move the objects in a cluster up to a short distance within range. You can choose to concentrate on this spell and hold your grasp on the objects until your next turn. During your next turn, you can cast this spell again to move the objects further. On a failure, you drop the objects at their current location.
Throw. Once per object, roll vs. Dodge against a target in range. You can choose the same or different targets for each thrown object. On a hit, each object deals 2 weapon damage in addition to your spell base damage. If you throw a weapon, apply it’s properties and effects in addition to the spell effect.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Choose up to four objects of up to 4 load each instead. Each thrown object deals 4 weapon damage.
(Rank 3) Choose up to five objects of up to 6 load each instead. Each thrown object deals 6 weapon damage.

name: Levitation
effect:
You push yourself against the pull of the earth, letting you hover visibly above the ground.
On a success, you start levitating. While levitating, you gain the following effects:
- You gain +1 boon on Agility + Stealth rolls to move silently.
- You can’t move more than a short distance per turn.
- You ignore the effects of difficult terrain from the ground below you.
- You don‘t trigger any mechanisms in the ground, such as pressure plates.
- When you would fall, you instead softly glide a short distance per round lower.
The levitation lasts for a medium duration. If the spell ends while you still fall, you fall the remaining distance as normal.

name: Orbiting Shards
effect: Five slivers of stone and metal tear loose and wheel about you in a slow, humming orbit, ready to turn a blow or leap at your command. You raise five shards that orbit you while you concentrate, for up to a medium duration. As a Quick Action when you are attacked, expend one shard to gain +5 AV against that attack. As your Action, hurl any number of remaining shards: make one Arcana attack roll vs. Dodge and divide the hurled shards among any creatures in short range, each shard dealing +2 damage to its target on a hit. When the last shard is spent or lost, the spell ends.
heightened:
(Rank 4) The block grants +6 AV and each shard deals +3 damage instead.
(Rank 5) The block grants +7 AV and each shard deals +4 damage instead.

name: Stasis
effect:
You pressure a creature in kinetic force from all sides, making them unable to move. You suffer +1 bane on the roll for every size category the target is larger than you.
On a success, the target is stunned while you concentrate on this spell. If the target takes damage while stunned by this spell, you automatically lose concentration for it and the effect ends.
You can use your Action on your next turn to repeat the roll for this spell without spending additional Focus. On a success, continue the spell’s effect. On a failure or if you choose not to use your Action to continue the spell, the spell ends and you can’t continue to cast it for free.

name: Strong Telekinesis
effect:
You hold out your hand and grasp at a large object in range, slowly levitating it it into the air. The object can be of large or smaller size and take up to 10 x your carrying capacity in load. The object can be any loose object lying around, or ammo, a weapon, or another item you carry.
You can choose to either move or throw the object towards a target location.
Move. Roll vs. very hard difficulty (12). On a success, you move the object up to a short distance within range. You can choose to concentrate on this spell and hold your grasp on the object until your next turn. During your next turn, you can cast this spell again to move the object further. On a failure, you drop the object at it’s current location.
Throw. Roll vs. Dodge against a single target in range. On a hit, the object deals 6 weapon damage in addition to your spell base damage. If you throw a weapon, apply it’s properties and effects in addition to the spell effect.
heightened:
(Rank 3) The object can be of huge or smaller size and take up to 20 x your carrying capacity in load. A thrown object deals 8 weapon damage.
(Rank 4) The object can be of gargantuan or smaller size and take up to 30 x your carrying capacity in load. A thrown object deals 10 weapon damage.
(Rank 5) The object can be of gargantuan or smaller size and take up to 50 x your carrying capacity in load. You can move it up to a medium distance within range. A thrown object deals 12 weapon damage.

name: Distortion Field
effect: The air within warps like heat over sand, and anything crossing it is shoved from its true course. On a success, you create a distortion zone in short range of the target location for up to a medium duration while you concentrate. Ranged attacks that pass into or through the zone suffer +1 bane, and the zone is difficult terrain for your enemies. When a creature enters the zone for the first time on their turn or starts their turn within it, they must succeed on an Agility + Fortitude roll vs. your Resist or take 6 damage (ignore AV).

name: Reverse Gravity
effect: You seize the pull of the earth itself and turn it on its head, and everything not rooted down plunges toward the sky. On a success, gravity reverses within a cylinder in short range of the target location, rising to a height of up to medium range, for up to a short duration while you concentrate. Each creature and unsecured object in the cylinder falls upward toward its top. A creature may succeed on an Agility + Athletics roll vs. your Resist to grab hold of a fixed object they can reach and avoid the rise. A creature or object that strikes a ceiling or an anchored object as it rises takes falling damage for the distance risen. A creature that reaches the top of the cylinder without striking anything hovers there until the spell ends, when they fall back down and take falling damage for the distance fallen.
heightened: (Rank 5) The cylinder's radius extends to medium range and its height to long range of the target location instead.

name: Drain Life
effect:
You inflict the target with a spectral necrotic energy, draining their very life force.
Weak. Deal +2 necrotic damage.
Strong. Deal +4 necrotic damage and regain +0 HP.
Critical. Deal +6 necrotic damage and regain +2 HP.

name: Enfeebling Grasp
effect:
You summon a ghostly translucent claw, taking hold of the target and draining their strength.
Weak. Deal +2 necrotic damage.
Strong. Deal +4 necrotic damage and briefly reduce their Strength die by one step.
Critical. Deal +6 necrotic damage and reduce their Strength die by one step for a short duration.
They can roll Spirit + Fortitude at the end of their turns to end the effect early.
Lowering an Attribute die temporarily doesn't change any statistics derived from it. Any individual creature can only suffer this effect once at a time.

name: Life Echo
effect:
You pry the mind of one deceased creature with an intact head, that died no further back than one week.
Weak. You gain one insight.
Strong. You gain two insights.
Critical. You gain three insights.
You can use one insight for specific information about the deceased creatures thoughts, emotions, or knowledge before death. Ask one question per insight and the GM will answer truthfully with a single word per question.
You can only cast this spell once per deceased creature.

name: Necromantic Sight
effect:
Your eyes take on a spectral glow as you perceive the boundaries between life and death.
On a success, you briefly gain the ability to see undead creatures, corpses, and ethereal spirits through walls and obstacles within medium range. You can identify their general nature (size, form, how long dead, approximate strength) but not specific details.

name: Soul Drain
effect:
You reach out with spectral tendrils to siphon away fragments of a creature's soul essence, weakening their spirit.
Weak. Deal +2 necrotic damage and briefly reduce their Spirit die by one step.
Strong. Deal +4 necrotic damage and briefly reduce their Spirit die by one step.
Critical. Deal +6 necrotic damage and reduce their Spirit die by one step for a short duration.
They can roll Spirit + Fortitude at the end of their turns to end the attribute reduction early.
Lowering an Attribute die temporarily doesn't change any statistics derived from it. Any individual creature can only suffer this effect once at a time.

name: Animate Corpse
effect:
You sacrifice your own life force to animate the corpse of a creature with medium or smaller size in range as an undead creature. The creature’s remains must not be missing any crucial body parts for it to be animated. You can choose to either animate them in the form of a zombie or skeleton (see the undead companion traits under Mounts & Companions). If the remains are heavily decayed, you can only animate them as a skeleton. You can’t reanimate remains of undead creatures. The undead has the statistics of a tier 1 companion. In addition, it gains a companion trait, according to which form you have chosen. You can equip the undead creature with weapons.
On a success, reduce your current and maximum HP by 4 and reanimate the remains under your control (treat the creature as a companion for combat).
You can dismiss the creature as a Quick Action during your turn. Otherwise it remains animated until reduced to 0 HP.
If you lose concentration on this spell, the creature becomes hostile to you and your allies. Roll initiative for it and treat it as an enemy. You can’t dismiss the creature anymore and it remains until reduced to 0 HP. After you stop controlling the undead creature, your maximum HP returns to normal.
You can choose to forgo the benefits of a night’s rest to solidify your control over the undead creature. After that rest and without breaking concentration, the undead creature remains animated and under your control without you concentrating on the spell anymore. Casting this spell again on another corpse will destroy the previous undead creature.
heightened:
(Rank 2) You can target up to large corpses and the undead creature has the statistics of a tier 2 creature instead. Also, reduce your HP by 6 instead.
(Rank 3) You can target up to huge corpses and the undead creature has the statistics of a tier 3 creature instead. Also, reduce your HP by 8 instead.

name: Bone Armor
effect:
You harvest bone fragments from a corpse to create protective armor around yourself.
You must be within touching distance of a corpse of medium or larger size with most of it’s larger bones intact.
On a success, the bones transform into different armor parts that attach themselves to your body. While the bone armor holds, you gain +3 AV (armor bonus) and resistance against necrotic damage. After taking 10 or more damage from a single hit, spend 1 use for the bone armor. Once you spend all three uses of the bone armor, it shatters and the spell ends. The bone armor lasts for a long duration. You can only cast this spell once per corpse.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The target gains +4 AV (armor bonus) and the bone armor only loses a use from 15 or more damage form a single hit instead.
(Rank 3) The target gains +5 AV (armor bonus) and the bone armor only loses a use from 20 or more damage form a single hit instead.

name: Control Undead
effect:
You dominate the evil spirit in one undead creature you can see within range. The target has to Tier 1 or lower.
Weak. The target is briefly stunned.
Strong. You briefly take control of the target, choosing what they do on their next turn.
Critical. You take control of the target for a short duration, choosing what they do on their turns.
They can roll Spirit + Fortitude at the end of their turns to end the effect early. Once they do, you can no longer cast this spell against them for the rest of the scene.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The target can be Tier 3 or lower instead.
(Rank 3) The target can be Tier 5 or lower instead.

name: Death Bolt
effect:
You conjure a bolt of pure negative energy and hurl it against the target.
Weak. Deal +4 necrotic damage.
Strong. Deal +8 necrotic damage.
Critical. Deal +12 necrotic damage.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +6/+12/+18 necrotic damage instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +8/+16/+24 necrotic damage instead.

name: Death Ward
effect:
You channel negative energy to protect a creature from harm at the cost of their own life force. Cast this spell when you or a willing creature within range takes damage.
On a success, the target immediately suffers one Fatigue but they only take half of the damage.

name: Grasp of Decay
effect:
You envelop your target in ethereal, necrotic energy slowly draining their life.
Weak. Deal +3 necrotic damage and they take 2 lasting necrotic damage for a short duration.
Strong. Deal +6 necrotic damage and they take 4 lasting necrotic damage for a short duration.
Critical. Deal +9 necrotic damage they take 6 lasting necrotic damage for a short duration.
They can roll Spirit + Fortitude at the end of their turns to end the effect early.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +4/+8/+12 necrotic damage instead and the lasting damage increases to 3/6/9.
(Rank 3) Deal +5/+10/+15 necrotic damage instead and the lasting damage increases to 4/8/12.

name: Necrotic Shield
effect:
You draw life energy from the environment around you, converting it into a protective barrier.
On a success, deal 2 necrotic damage (ignore AV) to yourself and all creatures in close range. Any smaller plant life within range withers and dies as their life force is drained.
The necrotic shield charges 6 HP + the total amount of HP drained.
When you take damage, the necrotic shield absorbs it first. It has resistance to necrotic and weakness to radiant damage. The shield lasts for a short duration or until it’s charged HP are lost.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal 4 necrotic damage (ignore AV) instead. The necrotic shield charges 12 HP + the total amount of HP drained instead.
(Rank 3) Deal 6 necrotic damage (ignore AV) instead. The necrotic shield charges 18 HP + the total amount of HP drained instead.

name: Ray of Lethargy
effect:
You extend your hand, unleashing a thin beam of necrotic energy that saps the vigor from your foe.
Weak. Deal +2 necrotic damage and the target is briefly slowed.
Strong. Deal +4 necrotic damage and the target is slowed for a short duration.
Critical. Deal +6 necrotic damage and the target is briefly staggered and slowed for a short duration.
At the end of their turns, they can roll Strength + Fortitude vs. your Resist to end the effect early.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +4/+8/+12 damage instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +6/+12/+18 damage instead.

name: Animate Horde
effect:
With an incantation of ancient power, you call forth a dark surge of necromantic energy that animates multiple corpses to rise as loyal undead servants under your command.
This spell works the same way as the Animate Corpse spell, but you can target up to two corpses in range to animate as undead with tier 1 statistics instead. You must choose the same type of undead for all creatures animated by this spell.
You also have to reduce your current and max. HP by 4 for each undead creature animated this way.
You can only give the horde of undead the same commands, not different ones to singular creatures within the horde. In combat, they always act as a troop if their numbers are large enough (three or more).
After forgoing a night’s rest, the entire horde remains under your control without requiring concentration on this spell.
heightened:
(Rank 2) You can animate up to three corpses instead.
(Rank 3) You can animate up to four corpses instead.

name: Bone Shatter
effect:
You target a creature's skeletal structure with necromantic energy, causing micro-fractures and bone weakness.
Weak. Deal +4 damage and the target is briefly staggered.
Strong. Deal +8 damage and the target is briefly stunned.
Critical. Deal +12 damage and the target is briefly paralyzed.
You can only target any individual creature once per scene with this spell.
heightened: (Rank 3) Deal +6/+12/+18 damage instead.

name: Corpse Explosion
effect:
You cause a corpse to violently explode in a burst of necrotic energy and bone fragments.
Target any mostly intact corpse of at least small Size in range. On a success, the corpse explodes, target all creatures in close range of it. For a corpse with at least large Size, target all creatures in short range instead. Use your roll’s result vs. their Dodge.
Weak. Deal +6 damage against each target.
Strong. Deal +12 damage against each target and they are knocked prone.
Critical. Deal +18 damage against each target and they are knocked prone.
The explosion destroys the corpse completely, making it unusable for any necromantic spells or effects.
heightened: (Rank 3) The explosion deals +8/+16/+24 damage to all targets instead.

name: Death Mark
effect:
You place a spectral mark of death upon a creature, making them vulnerable to necromantic energies.
On a success, the target suffers the death mark for a short duration. The mark is visible as a faint skull sigil on their forehead. It inflicts the following effects:
- Attacks against them gain +1 boon.
- Each time they take damage, also inflict 4 necrotic damage (ignore AV) to them.
- If the marked creature dies while under this effect, you regain Focus equal to half the spell's cost.
They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the mark early.
heightened: (Rank 3) You can target up to two creatures in range with the spell instead.

name: Inflict Curse
effect:
With a malevolent chant and a wave of your hand, you cast a powerful curse upon a target, subjecting them to a lingering maleficent influence.
Choose one of their attributes (Strength, Agility, Spirit, or Mind).
On a success, the target suffers the following effects:
- Their attribute die for the chosen attribute is reduced by one step.
- They suffer +1 bane on any attack rolls against you.
- You gain +1 boon on attacks against the target.
The curse lasts for a long duration. Lowering an Attribute die temporarily doesn't change any statistics derived from it.
heightened: (Rank 3) You can reduce two different attributes by one step each, or reduce one attribute by two steps instead.

name: Shroud of Blight
effect:
You summon a shroud of necrotic energy, inflicting fear on any creature within it. The shroud extends in a close area around the target location and targets all living creatures within it.
Weak. Deal +3 necrotic damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +6 necrotic damage to each target and they are briefly frightened by the shroud.
Critical. Deal +9 necrotic damage to each target and they are briefly frightened by the shroud.
Any living creature attempting to enter the shroud for the first time on their turns has to roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist. On a failure, they take +6 necrotic damage and are overwhelmed by intense fear are briefly frightened by the shroud, and instantly have to roll their Fear check.
The shroud lasts for a short duration. It can also be expelled early by any effect causing strong winds in the same area of influence.
heightened: (Rank 3) Deal +4/+8/+12 necrotic damage and creatures entering the shroud take +8 necrotic damage instead.

name: Soul Veil
effect:
You shroud your life force with an illusion of death, making yourself harder to detect and target.
On a success, you gain the following benefits for a short duration:
- You gain +1 boons on Stealth checks.
- Creatures attempting to target you with a spell suffer +1 bane on their spellcasting roll.
- Undead creatures treat you as one of their own and won't attack you unless you attack them first.
- You appear as a corpse visually and to magical detection that reveals life force or living creatures.
heightened: (Rank 3) You can choose any willing creatures in your melee range to also gain the benefits of this spell.

name: Curse of Mortality
effect:
You afflict a creature with a specialized curse that makes them vulnerable to disease and accelerates their aging.
On a success, the target suffers the following effects:
- They are deprived and can’t remove this condition by eating, drinking, or sleeping.
- They lose any immunity or resistance to disease, being poisoned, or poison damage.
They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the curse early. If they are unable to end the curse for a short duration, it becomes permanent without you having to concentrate on it. It can then only be removed by magical means, such as the Remove Curse spell.

name: Negative Energy Flood
effect:
You release a torrent of pure negative energy that devastates living creatures while empowering the undead. Target all living creatures in the spell's area.
Weak. Each target takes +4 necrotic damage.
Strong. Each target takes +8 necrotic damage and is briefly shaken.
Critical. Each target takes +12 necrotic damage and and is briefly stunned.
Plant life in the area immediately withers and dies. Any living creatures being killed by this spell animate as tier 1 zombies (see the companion rules for more details) under your control at the start of your next turn and last for a short duration (they crumble to dust when the duration ends).

name: Reaper’s Harvest
effect:
You create a field of death that feeds on the life force of dying creatures, empowering your necromantic abilities.
Choose any point within range. On a success, you establish a necromantic field in a close area around the target location for a short duration.
Living creatures within the field suffer +1 bane on all rolls. Whenever a living creature dies within the field, you gain the following benefits:
- Regain 2 Focus.
- Regain 2 HP.
- Gain +1 boon on your next Mind + Arcana roll to cast a necromancy spell.

name: Soul Prison
effect:
You attempt to trap a creature's soul within spectral bindings, gradually separating it from their body.
The target must be living and Tier 5 or lower. If the creature is at full HP, you suffer +1 bane on the roll.
On a success, the target's soul begins to separate and is stunned.
At the end of their next turn, repeat the roll for this spell. If you succeed, the soul separation continues and they are now paralyzed.
At the end of their turn after that, repeat the roll again. If you succeed, their soul is imprisoned and their body become unconscious and unresponsive. Their soul manifests as a tiny almost weightless magical crystal within your palm.
The creature’s soul remains imprisoned while you remain concentrated on the spell and the crystal is intact. When the creature’s body takes damage during this time, make a Concentration roll as if you took the same amount of damage. After a medium duration concentrating on this spell, the soul imprisonment becomes permanent and has to be lifted by an effect that can break curses, restore souls, or by breaking the crystal.

name: Spectral Army
effect:
You summon spectral warriors from beyond the veil to fight alongside you.
